lessons.subject: Rational Numbers
lessons.title: What Are Rational Numbers?
lessons.body: A rational number is any number that can be written as a fraction a/b where a and b are integers and b ≠ 0. This includes integers, fractions, terminating decimals, and repeating decimals.

quiz.prompt: Which of these is a rational number?

lessons.subject: Rational Numbers
lessons.title: Terminating & Repeating Decimals
lessons.body: A decimal is rational if it ends (terminates) or repeats forever. For example, 0.5 and 0.333… are both rational.

lessons.subject: Rational Numbers
lessons.title: Converting Fractions to Decimals
lessons.body: To convert a fraction to a decimal, divide the numerator by the denominator. Example: 3/4 = 0.75.

lessons.subject: Rational Numbers
lessons.title: Converting Decimals to Fractions
lessons.body: Write the decimal over a power of 10 and simplify. Example: 0.6 = 6/10 = 3/5.

lessons.subject: Integers
lessons.title: Adding Integers
lessons.body: Same signs: add and keep the sign. Different signs: subtract and keep the sign of the larger absolute value.

lessons.subject: Integers
lessons.title: Subtracting Integers
lessons.body: Subtracting a number is the same as adding its opposite. Example: 7 - (-2) = 7 + 2 = 9.

lessons.subject: Integers
lessons.title: Multiplying Integers
lessons.body: Same signs → positive. Different signs → negative. Example: (-3)(4) = -12.

lessons.subject: Integers
lessons.title: Dividing Integers
lessons.body: Same signs → positive. Different signs → negative. Example: 12 ÷ (-3) = -4.

lessons.subject: Rational Operations
lessons.title: Adding Fractions
lessons.body: To add fractions, use a common denominator. Example: 1/4 + 1/6 = 5/12.

lessons.subject: Rational Operations
lessons.title: Subtracting Fractions
lessons.body: Use a common denominator and subtract numerators. Example: 5/8 - 1/4 = 3/8.

lessons.subject: Absolute Value
lessons.title: Understanding Absolute Value
lessons.body: Absolute value is the distance from zero. It is always non‑negative. Example: |-7| = 7.

lessons.subject: Absolute Value
lessons.title: Absolute Value in Context
lessons.body: Absolute value is used to measure distance, temperature change, and elevation differences.

quiz.prompt: A submarine is 300 ft below sea level. What is its distance from sea level?
